rationale.R1.1: The revision clarifies the framing of QuerySieve as a debiasing protocol and its target (length confound in LLM judging). It identifies the key issue (LLMs preferring longer SQL) and proposes a solution (regression adjustment).
rationale.R1.4: The text mentions prior work on using LLMs for SQL evaluation (Spider 2.0, BIRD) but doesn't explicitly contrast QuerySieve with them or other methods.
rationale.RX.1: The summary acknowledges uncertainty about the generalizability of the findings to other judges and benchmarks.
rationale.RX.3: The revised text stays within the scope of discussing QuerySieve and its application to SQL-Eval leaderboard.
rationale.RX.4: No harmful content or regressions are introduced in the revision.
rationale.RX.5: While the summary mentions changes made, it doesn't provide specific details about source attribution for each change.
